title: Add amenities field to hostels with display and edit support

requirements.text: Add an `amenities` field (array of Text) to the Hostel type in backend/main.mo. Update the `addHostel` function to accept and store an amenities array. Update the `updateHostel` function to also accept and update the amenities field. Add representative amenities to all existing seed hostel records (e.g. WiFi, AC, Laundry, Parking, Meals, Gym, Hot Water, Power Backup, Security, CCTV, Study Room, Common Kitchen — use a realistic subset per hostel type and category).

quotes: add each hostel amenities to this site for each hostel and edit option also

acceptanceCriteria: The Hostel type in backend/main.mo includes an `amenities : [Text]` field.
The `addHostel` function accepts an amenities array and stores it on the new hostel record.
The `updateHostel` function accepts an updated amenities array and persists it.
All existing seed hostel records have a non-empty amenities array with realistic values.
The backend compiles without errors and returns amenities when queried.

requirements.text: Update the HostelCard component (frontend/src/components/HostelCard.tsx) to display a compact row of amenity icons/pills below the price and sharing details. Show up to 4–5 amenity tags (e.g. WiFi, AC, Meals) as small badge-style pills using the warm amber/sage color palette. If there are more amenities than shown, display a '+N more' indicator. Only display the amenities row when the hostel has at least one amenity.

quotes: add each hostel amenities to this site for each hostel and edit option also

acceptanceCriteria: HostelCard shows up to 5 amenity pills below the price/sharing section.
Each pill is styled as a small badge using the existing warm color palette.
If more than 5 amenities exist, a '+N more' badge is displayed.
If a hostel has no amenities, the amenities row is not rendered.
The card layout does not break on mobile or desktop.

requirements.text: Update the HostelDetail page (frontend/src/pages/HostelDetail.tsx) to display a dedicated 'Amenities' section that lists all amenities for the hostel. Each amenity should be shown as an icon+label pill or badge. Use appropriate icons (e.g. WiFi icon for WiFi, snowflake for AC, fork-and-knife for Meals, car for Parking, dumbbell for Gym). Style the section consistently with the existing warm terracotta/sage/amber color palette, placed between the room sharing/pricing section and the map section.

quotes: add each hostel amenities to this site for each hostel and edit option also

acceptanceCriteria: HostelDetail page has a clearly labelled 'Amenities' section.
All amenities for the hostel are displayed as icon+label pills.
The section is placed between room sharing/pricing and the map.
If a hostel has no amenities, the section is hidden or shows 'No amenities listed'.
The section is fully responsive on both mobile and desktop.

requirements.text: Update the EditHostel page (frontend/src/pages/EditHostel.tsx) to include an amenities editing section. Display a checklist or multi-select input of common amenities (WiFi, AC, Laundry, Parking, Meals, Gym, Hot Water, Power Backup, Security, CCTV, Study Room, Common Kitchen). Pre-populate the checkboxes with the hostel's currently stored amenities. Allow the user to check/uncheck amenities. Include the updated amenities array in the form submission payload sent to the backend `updateHostel` function.

quotes: add each hostel amenities to this site for each hostel and edit option also

acceptanceCriteria: EditHostel page includes a checklist of at least 12 common amenities.
Checkboxes are pre-populated with the hostel's current amenities when the page loads.
Users can check and uncheck amenities freely.
On form submission, the updated amenities array is sent to the backend `updateHostel` function.
Success toast is shown after saving and the user is returned to the hostel detail page.
The amenities section is styled consistently with the rest of the EditHostel form.

requirements.text: Update the mutation hook in frontend/src/hooks/useMutations.ts so that the `useUpdateHostel` mutation passes the amenities field (alongside the existing roomSharing data) to the backend `updateHostel` function call.
acceptanceCriteria: The `useUpdateHostel` mutation hook includes amenities in the update payload.
The hook compiles without TypeScript errors.
Calling the hook with an updated amenities array correctly updates the hostel in the backend.

constraints: Do not change existing immutable frontend paths (hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, hooks/useActor.ts, main.tsx, components/ui).
All Motoko logic must remain in the single backend/main.mo actor file.
Amenities must be stored as an array of Text strings in the backend — no separate data structure required.
nonGoals: Adding a dedicated amenities management admin page beyond the EditHostel form.
User-defined custom amenity creation (only predefined common amenities).
Filtering hostels by amenities on the Home page (not requested).
Changing the AddHostel multi-step form flow beyond adding amenities checkbox step.
